data.narrative:
Vatn Systems is a Series A defense and security technology company founded by Nelson Mills, currently valued at $300M post-money on $76.5M raised. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 10 firms spanning the same sector and stage cohort: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Vannevar Labs, Anduril Industries, and Castelion.

Two triangulation methods apply to this stage. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x multiplier to the $300M last post-money valuation, yielding $600M, a typical step-up for Series A to successor rounds. Peer-median analysis across 9 same-sector comparables—predominantly Series B companies with valuations ranging $60M to $1.5B—establishes a median of $1.0B. The peer set includes five representatively-sized firms valued between $1.0B and $1.5B at Series B.

Triangulating these two approaches produces a $510M low, $800M median, and $1.1B high valuation range. The $290M spread between endpoints reflects meaningful uncertainty around whether Vatn's execution and go-to-market strength align with the higher-valued peer median or the more conservative stage-progression floor. The current $300M valuation sits materially below the derived range, suggesting either valuation conservatism at close or that subsequent data on unit economics or customer concentration has not yet been incorporated into market pricing.
